summary: P1 batch 2 (SEO + sitemap + image uploads + robots.txt) verified. Backend: 12 NEW tests pass (52/52 total, 100%). Frontend: SEO meta tags applied via react-helmet-async on all 9 public pages (Home, About, Services, Shop, ShopDetail, Blog, BlogDetail, Booking, Contact) with unique <title>, og:title (per-page), og:image, og:description, twitter:card, canonical link. ShopDetail/BlogDetail correctly use product/post name. ImageUploader works on AdminDashboard for Blog cover, Testimonial photo, and Product cover — file picker uploads, returns absolute https://.../api/uploads/<path>, preview shown via toast 'Image uploaded', Replace + Remove buttons functional, manual URL <details> fallback present. robots.txt served at /robots.txt with 'Disallow: /admin' and 'Sitemap: /api/sitemap.xml'. /api/sitemap.xml returns valid XML using https + host header (no localhost), includes 7 main pages + 3 blog slugs + 4 product detail URLs.
action_items: (carry-over from iter 2, not in P1-batch-2 scope) Wrap admin Copy-link clipboard write in try/catch with fallback to avoid CRA dev redbox on permission-denied browsers.

(nice-to-have) Sitemap currently uses today's date as <lastmod> for static paths and falls back to today for missing published_at — acceptable, but consider tracking real updated_at on blog posts for more accurate SEO signals.
(nice-to-have) /api/sitemap.xml omits trailing slash on the homepage <loc> (renders as 'https://host/'). That is correct. No change required.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py /api/sitemap.xml correctly forces https and honors x-forwarded-proto/x-forwarded-host — verified no internal hostnames leak.

server.py admin_upload_image reads the entire body before checking size — for a malicious 100MB upload this still buffers all bytes in memory. Consider using `file.size` (UploadFile property) or streaming with a running counter to fail fast. Functional today but a DoS surface.
server.py serve_upload returns Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400 — good; consider also adding ETag based on storage_path for revalidation.

server.py /api/admin/uploads/image returns relative URL '/api/uploads/<path>' but the frontend ImageUploader prefixes window.location.origin to make it absolute. That's fine, but for clients consuming the API directly (mobile, etc.) returning an already-absolute URL would be more robust.
SEO.jsx: window.location.origin is read at render time — works for CRA SPA, but for SSR/prerender (if added later) this would yield empty canonical. Not a problem today.
Carry-over from iter 1: Admin product modal still missing data-testid on individual fields (name/description/price/category).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_p1_seo_uploads.py (NEW — 12 tests for sitemap, image upload, file serve, robots.txt)
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Tests use existing seeded blog posts + products. Image upload tests create transient uploaded_files records (not cleaned up — minor; can be filtered by 'TEST_' if needed in future).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=<url> pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. P1-batch-2 (SEO + uploads + robots) fully verified end-to-end. ImageUploader testid pattern: admin-{blog-cover|testimonial-photo|product-cover}-{pick,input,url,replace,remove}. Uploader returns full https URL like 'https://<host>/api/uploads/blooming-branch/uploads/images/<uuid>.<ext>'. /api/sitemap.xml is at the API path; /robots.txt is served from the frontend root and references /api/sitemap.xml.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no failures. All P1-batch-2 acceptance criteria met. Existing flows (home/blog/admin/orders-reissue/Stripe checkout-status) remain green.
